title: stylidium spathulatum
intro: Stylidium spathulatum, also known as the triggerplant, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia. It is a member of the Stylidiaceae family and is known for its unique trigger-like mechanism that releases nectar when touched. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The triggerplant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to take advantage of its health benefits, such as reducing fever and promoting wound healing.
